entries.position_summary: The country condemns the DPRK’s ongoing unlawful nuclear and ballistic missile programs, viewing them as a threat to international peace and security and to the global nonproliferation regime.
entries.quote: The DPRK continues its unlawful nuclear and ballistic missile programs, threatening international peace and security and the global nonproliferation regime.

entries.position_summary: The United States condemns the DPRK’s unlawful nuclear and missile programs and its threatening rhetoric, viewing them as a serious threat to international peace and the nonproliferation regime. It seeks complete, verifiable denuclearization of the Korean Peninsula through sustained diplomacy and insists on the DPRK’s return to the NPT and IAEA safeguards.
entries.quote: Chair, The DPRK’s unlawful and continuing nuclear and ballistic missile programs constitute a serious and direct threat to international peace and security and to the global nonproliferation regime. The DPRK’s threatening and irresponsible rhetoric has also intensified, including by characterizing some of its missile launches and other military activities as “trial runs” for the use of tactical nuclear weapons. Our goal remains the complete and verifiable denuclearization of the Korean Peninsula. In that regard, the United States is committed to serious, calibrated, and sustained diplomacy with the DPRK, and we will continue to insist on its return to the NPT and IAEA safeguards.

entries.position_summary: The country expresses concern over Iranian officials' statements implying a capability to acquire nuclear weapons and calls for rejecting such rhetoric. It urges collective condemnation to deter proliferation-related threats.
entries.quote: Concerning Iran, we note the recent statements by Iranian officials signaling a capability to acquire nuclear weapons should it decide to do so. We must all join in rejecting such language.

entries.position_summary: The statement condemns Russia’s war against Ukraine and its irresponsible nuclear rhetoric, criticizes reckless actions at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant as endangering nuclear facilities and safety, and rejects Russia’s claimed suspension of New START as contrary to international law.
entries.quote: Russia’s unprovoked war against Ukraine tragically continues, as do Russia’s irresponsible nuclear rhetoric, its reckless actions at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, and its claim to suspend the New START Treaty, a claim that is inconsistent with international law.

entries.position_summary: The country calls for immediate assistance to any NPT non-nuclear-weapon State that faces nuclear threats or aggression involving nuclear weapons. This reflects support for strong security assurances in such circumstances.
entries.quote: • Third, the necessity of providing or supporting immediate assistance to any non-nuclear weapon State Party to the NPT that is an object of a threat or ofact of aggression involving the use of nuclear weapons.

entries.position_summary: The United States condemns Russia for violating the Budapest Memorandum’s security assurances to Ukraine and for irresponsible nuclear rhetoric and WMD disinformation, which heighten risks and undermine disarmament efforts. The U.S. pledges not to engage in such behavior and to uphold its own security assurances.
entries.quote: The international community cannot, and should not, ignore Russia’s repeated and flagrant disregard for its international obligations and commitments, including obligations under the UN Charter. Russia’s horrific war against Ukraine has betrayed the very security assurances Russia gave to Ukraine through the Budapest Memorandum when Ukraine joined the NPT. Russia’s irresponsible rhetoric regarding nuclear weapons, disinformation about possible WMD use, and past use of chemical weapons further undermine efforts to make progress on disarmament and increase the risk of miscalculation. The United States will not engage in such irresponsible and dangerous behavior, and we will continue to uphold the assurances hawse have provided on our own part.

entries.position_summary: The United States views the DPRK’s missile and WMD programs as significant security threats and calls for the complete denuclearization of North Korea. It urges the DPRK to return to the NPT and to compliance with IAEA safeguards.
entries.quote: Similarly, the DPRK’s ballis�c missile and weapons of mass destruc�on programs pose significant security threats. The United States calls for the complete denucleariza�on of North Korea and its return to the NPT and IAEA

entries.position_summary: The country condemns Russia’s actions in Ukraine, accusing it of seizing peaceful nuclear facilities and engaging in reckless nuclear rhetoric. It also asserts that Russia has violated provisions of the New START Treaty.
entries.quote: • Russia, an NPT nuclear-weapon state and treaty depository, is engaged in an illegal war against its non-nuclear weapon state neighbor. It has violently seized Ukraine’s peaceful nuclear facilities; engaged in reckless nuclear rhetoric; violated provisions of the New START Treaty; and may be

entries.position_summary: The statement criticizes China’s rapid, opaque nuclear buildup and rejects China’s proposed No First Use treaty as non-credible and unverifiable. It also condemns Russia’s coercive nuclear rhetoric and highlights concern over Russia’s increased military cooperation with the DPRK.
entries.quote: Such falsehoods distract from the truly worrisome issues at hand, including China’s rapid and opaque nuclear weapons build-up, and Russia’s coercive nuclear rhetoric and increased military cooperation with the DPRK. We were dismayed by some States Parties’ support for China’s proposal for a non- credible, unverifiable No First Use Treaty. We must ask – what good is a statement from China that it will not use nuclear weapons first, when its nuclear build-up appears to contradict to its stated policy?

entries.position_summary: The statement condemns Russia for qualitatively expanding its nuclear forces, undermining arms control by purporting to suspend New START, and engaging in irresponsible nuclear rhetoric. It also accuses Russia of violating its security assurances to Ukraine.
entries.quote: Russia has developed novel nuclear weapons and improved dual-capable delivery systems, unlawfully purported to suspend the New START Treaty, engaged in irresponsible nuclear rhetoric, and reneged on the security assurances it provided Ukraine.

entries.position_summary: The U.S. says the fundamental role of its nuclear weapons is to deter nuclear attack against itself, its allies, and partners, reflecting extended deterrence and a restrained role. It would consider nuclear use only in extreme circumstances to defend vital interests, indicating it does not adopt a no-first-use pledge. It rejects threatening nuclear use for intimidation or expansionist aims.
entries.quote: As long as nuclear weapons exist, the fundamental role of U.S. nuclear weapons is to deter nuclear attack on the United States, our allies, and our partners. The United States would consider the use of nuclear weapons only in extreme circumstances to defend the vital interests of the United States or its allies and partners. Unlike some others, we will not threaten the use of nuclear weapons to intimidate or to pursue an expansionist security policy.

entries.position_summary: The statement condemns Russia for violating its security assurances to Ukraine under the Budapest Memorandum and criticizes its purported suspension of New START and irresponsible nuclear rhetoric. It argues these actions increase nuclear risks and the chances of miscalculation, contrasting them with P3 efforts to engage responsibly on negative security assurances.
entries.quote: Unfortunately, in contrast to P3 efforts to engage responsibly on negative security assurances, the Russian Federation has disregarded the commitments and assurances it made in the Budapest Memorandum to respect the independence, sovereignty, and territorial integrity of Ukraine. Russia’s legally invalid, purported suspension of the New START Treaty and irresponsible rhetoric regarding nuclear weapons not only increase nuclear risks, but also heighten the possibility of miscalculation.

entries.position_summary: The United States denies responsibility for destabilizing actions and implicitly condemns Russia for invading a neighbor, undermining decades of bilateral arms control progress, engaging in dangerous nuclear rhetoric, endangering civilian nuclear facilities, and moving nuclear weapons closer to others’ borders in the context of the war in Ukraine.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, It is not the United States that has attacked a neighboring country without provocation. It is not the United States that has undermined a half century of progress on bilateral arms control, engaged in irresponsible and dangerous nuclear rhetoric, endangered civilian nuclear energy facilities, or moved nuclear weapons closer to others’ borders.

entries.position_summary: The country condemns the DPRK’s ongoing WMD and ballistic missile programs as violations of UN Security Council resolutions and a threat to regional and global security. It expresses strong concern over the DPRK’s continued nuclear activities and the possibility of a seventh nuclear test, as well as its bellicose rhetoric.
entries.quote: Meanwhile, Chair, the DPRK continues to advance its destabilizing weapons of mass destruction and ballistic missile programs in violation of multiple UN Security Council resolutions. These programs pose a threat to all of us and undermine security in the region. We note with significant concern the DPRK’s ongoing nuclear activities, including the possibility of a seventh nuclear test, and its continued irresponsible and bellicose rhetoric.

entries.position_summary: The country condemns the DPRK’s escalatory rhetoric and missile activities, noting Pyongyang’s portrayal of launches as trial runs for tactical nuclear weapon use. It also criticizes the DPRK’s malicious cyber activities used to finance and advance its unlawful weapons programs.
entries.quote: 2022. Pyongyang’s rhetoric has become increasingly escalatory and aggressive, going so far as to characterize certain of its missile launches and other military activities as trial runs for the use of tactical nuclear weapons. And it continues to generate revenue through malicious cyber activities that target all countries to further advance its unlawful weapons programs.

entries.position_summary: The country presses Russia to uphold arms control and pursue risk reduction, rejecting Russia’s purported suspension of New START as legally invalid and condemning its nuclear rhetoric for heightening risks of miscalculation. It also criticizes China’s rapid and opaque nuclear buildup, calling into question Beijing’s transparency and intentions.
entries.quote: We continue to press Russia on arms control and risk reduction despite its legally invalid purported suspension of the New START Treaty and its rhetoric regarding nuclear weapons that not only increase nuclear risks but also heighten the possibility of miscalculation. Meanwhile, China’s rapid and opaque nuclear weapons build-up raises questions about Beijing’s 2

entries.position_summary: The United States emphasizes addressing the threat from Russian and Chinese nuclear arsenals while showcasing its own transparency by publicly releasing stockpile numbers. It criticizes China and Russia for opaque build-ups and failing to reciprocate on transparency.
entries.quote: He has made clear his desire to address the threat posed by Russia and China’s nuclear arsenals. In sharp contrast to China’s opaque nuclear weapons build-up, the United States has been remarkably transparent, releasing the number of weapons in our stockpile twice in the last four years. However, for too long U.S. leadership and transparency have not been met with reciprocity by China and Russia.

entries.position_summary: The delegation notes that the U.S. and Israel’s Prime Minister repudiated an Israeli minister’s rhetoric, but criticizes other states for ignoring Russia’s irresponsible nuclear rhetoric related to its war against Ukraine. It implicitly condemns nuclear threats and calls out selective attention to such rhetoric.
entries.quote: With regard to comments by an Israeli minister, it is the case that both the United States and Israel’s Prime Minister repudiated use of such language. Regrettably, we cannot help but note that states highlighting this matter have been silent this week on Russia’s numerous and supremely irresponsible nuclear rhetoric in connection with its war against Ukraine.
